dependencies: Sole-source suppliers for certain raw materials and components
Single supplier qualification due to quality assurance, cost or regulatory requirements
Reliance on third-party sterilization providers including ethylene oxide sterilization
Concentration of manufacturing for certain product lines in one or few plants
risks: Supply interruption, reduction or termination from sole-source suppliers
Regulatory requirements for supplier qualification causing delays (3-18 months without clinical trials, 1-3 years with clinical trials)
Shortages in supply or disruptions in production and shipments of materials or components
Price increases and supply disruptions due to inflationary pressures, supplier capacity constraints, labor disruptions, transportation delays, geopolitical developments
Ethylene oxide sterilization regulatory restrictions and capacity limitations
Inability to transition manufacturing or sterilization to other sites in timely manner
Supplier shutdowns, insolvencies, extreme weather events, sanctions and trade restrictions

regulatory_risks: FDA regulation and oversight of medical devices including 510(k) clearance, PMA approval requirements
Consent Decree with FDA for BD Alaris infusion pumps with potential for manufacturing cessation, product recalls, and penalties up to $15 million per year
FDA Warning Letters for BD Preanalytical Systems and BD Pyxis dispensing equipment with potential for seizure, injunction, civil monetary penalties
EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR) and In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (IVDR) with stricter requirements and limited notified body capacity
Privacy and data protection laws (GDPR and similar) with significant compliance costs and penalties based on global revenue
Environmental regulations including EPA NESHAP for ethylene oxide emissions with 2-year compliance timeline
EPA Pesticide Registration Review for ethylene oxide use as sterilant
State environmental regulations for ethylene oxide use and emissions
Italian medical device payback legislation requiring payments to government
Healthcare compliance regulations including anti-kickback laws, False Claims Act, Medicare/Medicaid requirements
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and similar foreign anti-corruption laws
Economic sanctions and export control laws restricting business with certain countries, governments, entities
Sunshine Act provisions requiring public reporting of physician payments
Climate change and greenhouse gas emission regulations including Pillar Two global minimum tax
Product registration, labeling, and prohibited materials requirements in various jurisdictions
Tax law changes including OECD Pillar Two 15% minimum tax effective 2025

sanction_risks: U.S. and foreign economic sanctions restricting transactions with certain countries, governments, entities and individuals
Potential violations subject to government investigations, criminal or civil sanctions, exclusion from healthcare programs
Impact of evolving situations in Ukraine, Middle East and Asia on operations
Sanctions compliance requirements affecting international operations
